reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 1 (Dead Code): PASS - Function actively used in sync paths (block_verification.rs:652, sync_methods.rs:601). Gate 2 (Trust Boundary): PASS - Attack via untrusted P2P peers (DataColumnsByRange/DataColumnsByRoot RPC). Gate 3 (Code Verification): PASS - Factually correct: RPC path verify_kzg_for_rpc_blocks (L446) performs ONLY KZG validation, while gossip path validate_data_column_sidecar_for_gossip (L502-507) enforces signature verification, inclusion proof, and ancestry checks. Gate 4 (Exploitability): PASS - Attacker-triggered. An attacker can respond to DataColumnsByRange with columns having valid KZG proofs but forged signatures or invalid merkle proofs, bypassing cryptographic authenticity checks. Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): PASS - 01e property PROP-6a4369e9-asm-001 (outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json, L642-657) requires 'forall msg from P2P: processed(msg) => fully_validated(msg) == true'. RPC columns violate this by skipping signature/inclusion/ancestry validation. Gate 6 (Scope Check): PASS - P2P vulnerabilities in-scope, Fusaka-specific PeerDAS code. Severity: HIGH matches threshold (>33% network impact if exploited at scale). An attacker can trigger this via DataColumnsByRange RPC by sending columns with valid KZG proofs but forged signatures/invalid merkle proofs, causing the node to mark invalid columns as available and potentially reconstruct or propagate them without detecting the forgery.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: P2P peers are untrusted; all P2P input must be fully validated before acceptance. (assertion: forall msg from P2P: processed(msg) => fully_validated(msg) == true)
